mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is already near zero, a further decline or persistence of ultra-low policy rates reduces discount rates and supports equity multiples, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not show higher 20-day forward SPX returns than non-triggered episodes, or if the signal is inverted with negative average returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above a low-labor-demand threshold signals weakening household income and recession risk, which depresses risk appetite and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE rise triggers are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average triggered return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent decline in RSAFS and HOUST captures weakening consumer spending and housing demand; the joint deterioration signals broad demand softness and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term is not stronger than either series alone, or if combined down-drawdowns fail to precede negative 5-day SPX returns in at least 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO relative to a stable labor market indicates stronger industrial output and operating leverage, which supports earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if sustained INDPRO uptrends do not precede above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the effect disappears when PAYEMS is flat or rising.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is accelerating while FEDFUNDS remains unchanged, real policy rates fall and inflation uncertainty rises, compressing equity valuations and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-above-policy triggers are not followed by weaker 20-day SPX returns than comparable low-inflation periods, or if the sign turns positive for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS series indicates payroll contraction and weaker earnings power, which reduces forward equity returns over the next 5 trading days as investors price slower revenue growth.
failure_condition: Reject if negative PAYEMS prints are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the mean 5-day return is not below the unconditional benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in GDPC1 and RSAFS signals broad final-demand weakness across both output and spending, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations are revised down.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth negative and RSAFS 3-month change negative
failure_condition: Reject if the joint demand contraction does not outperform single-series signals, or if triggered 60-day SPX returns are not materially below returns in non-triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, rising INDPRO combined with stabilizing HOUST indicates supply-side normalization and inventory rebuild, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if recovery episodes with rising INDPRO and stable-to-rising HOUST do not exhibit higher 20-day SPX returns than the unconditional sample, or if the effect reverses in recovery windows.
